mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: SPX 20d forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC stays below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid monthly increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX 5d forward returns are not significantly negative when UNRATE spikes > 0.2pp month-over-month, or the signal's hit rate falls below 45% in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in residential construction starts and a sharp drop in consumer credit balances indicate a synchronized demand shock, which compresses equity multiples and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 20d returns more than 55% of the time, or the interaction term's coefficient in a multivariate regression is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in the industrial production index reflects robust manufacturing capacity utilization, which supports corporate profit margins and yields positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon during periods of stable inflation.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth shows no positive predictive power for 60d forward returns when CPIAUCSL is below 3.5%, or the signal reverses direction during high-inflation episodes.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0%, the cost of capital becomes sufficiently restrictive to compress equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following week as markets price in prolonged tight liquidity.
failure_condition: SPX 5d forward returns are not significantly negative when FEDFUNDS crosses above 5.0%, or the signal produces positive returns in more than 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reinforces expectations of durable economic expansion, supporting equity risk premiums and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no positive correlation with 60d forward returns over a 5-year rolling window, or the Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where real GDP growth accelerates while the CPI index decelerates indicates favorable supply-demand rebalancing, which expands equity risk appetite and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 20d returns when GDP growth exceeds 2.5% and CPI growth falls below 2.0%, or the interaction effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in industrial production signals an immediate supply-side shock or demand weakness, prompting rapid deleveraging in equity portfolios and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines do not lead to negative 5d forward returns more than 55% of the time, or the signal's predictive power disappears when UNRATE is below 4.0%.
